auditor: Claude Code (independent final audit, pre-edit snapshot)

inputs.audit_copy_construction: R7 full baseline + overlaid Stage B-R1 run_truth_gate.py and run_mutation_tests.py (per work order 1.2)

zhipu_claim_adjudication.reason: Independent replay reproduces 13/13 'BLOCKED' but 5 of them (m2,m5,m6,m9,m10) are FALSE GREEN: the detector's blanket 'except Exception: return False' converts SQL OperationalError / AttributeError / SyntaxError into 'BLOCKED', so the security invariant is not actually exercised. Additionally an honest default-mode replay exits 1 ('bundled report does not match current run') due to volatile build_id/duration fields, so the delivered evidence pack fails its own replay.

overall_conclusion_reason: All 12 known blockers are independently CONFIRMED. The R7 production baseline (app/daily_loop/services/*, migrations, tests) is sound and untouched; every defect lives in the allowed-to-modify evidence/detector/build layer (run_truth_gate.py, run_mutation_tests.py, build_release.py, reports). Therefore fixing is possible within the permitted scope without altering frozen production code.

findings.title: Mutation runner ignores semantic failure code
findings.evidence: Decision is `if exit_code == 1 and hits_match: result='BLOCKED'`. `observed_failure_code` is literally `f"exit {exit_code}"` (="exit 1"); `expected_failure_code` is stored but never compared. Any exit-1 (including exception-driven) counts as BLOCKED.

findings.title: m2 is an invalid-SQL mutation, not a cross-store semantic bypass
findings.evidence: m2 replaces `THEN RAISE(ABORT,'E-SCOPE...')` with `THEN SELECT 1` inside a CASE expr -> invalid SQL. Reproduced: cross_store detector logged `FAIL: schema init failed (trigger mutation detected): OperationalError`. Proves SQL broken, not that cross-store trigger semantics were gated.

findings.title: m6 trigger deletion only produces a SQL syntax error
findings.evidence: m6 comments only the `CREATE TRIGGER ...` line, leaving orphaned BEFORE/BEGIN/END body. Reproduced: `FAIL: schema init failed (trigger mutation detected): OperationalError`. Not a clean 'schema initializes, target trigger missing' detection; no E-CROSS-STORE-TRIGGER-MISSING semantic.

findings.title: m5 does not prove write-authorization bypass
findings.evidence: m5 replaces `self._authorize('write',ctx,store_id)` with `pass`. Reproduced: `FAIL: vault check exception (mutation detected): AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'member_id'` — ctx=None path raises first; never reaches staff/cross-store write. No E-VAULT-WRITE-AUTH-BYPASS; no proof unauthorized data persisted.

findings.title: m9 does not actually bypass rotate authorization
findings.evidence: m9 removes `self._require_context(ctx,'rotate')` but the following `self._authorize(...)` still enforces context/authorization. Reproduced: `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'store_id'`. No E-VAULT-ROTATE-AUTH-BYPASS; manager/cross-store rotate not shown to succeed.

findings.title: m10 is a syntax error, not a verifier mix
findings.evidence: m10 replaces `self._provider.verify(recovery_ctx)` with `True  # verifier mix` inside `if not <expr>:`, commenting out the trailing colon. Reproduced: `SyntaxError: expected ':' (vault_recovery_service.py, line 53)`. Provider verification never actually bypassed; no E-RECOVERY-PROVIDER-VERIFY-BYPASS.

findings.title: Vault detector treats any exception as a security FAIL (BLOCKED)
findings.evidence: check_vault wraps the whole gate in `except Exception as e: return False, 'vault check exception (mutation detected)...'` -> exit 1. SyntaxError/AttributeError/ImportError/env errors all become BLOCKED. The 24 vault items are not isolated; the first exception aborts the gate. This is the mechanism that makes m5/m9/m10 false-green.

findings.title: Security check bare-except false green
findings.evidence: check_security uses bare `except: pass` (lines 93,97) around Repository calls made with parameters that may not match the production API; a TypeError/AttributeError is swallowed and counted as 'security rejected'. Not calling real production signing with only expected exception types.

findings.title: Cross-store check swallows all exceptions
findings.evidence: check_cross_store uses bare `except: pass` on the semantic write; must accept only sqlite3.IntegrityError and assert the scope code. Also line 302 accepts `(PermissionError, TypeError)` as pass, admitting TypeError as a false green.

findings.title: Report fields not closed per work order
findings.evidence: observed_failure_code=='exit 1' (not semantic); MISSING started_at, ended_at, duration_ms, timed_out, signal; detector_command is a STRING not an argv array; normal-before/after carry only exit codes (normal_before_exit/normal_after_exit), no raw logs; no selector self-test result.

findings.title: Stage B-R1 evidence ZIP manifest not closed
findings.evidence: manifest lists 69 files; only 32 present on disk; 41 listed-but-absent; mutation_report.json present but unlisted (not in files, not in excluded). Missing STAGE_B_R1_PROVENANCE.md, selector self-test, stage-B detector matrix naming, the correction work order, and normal-before/after logs. Cannot serve as a closed evidence pack.

findings.title: Default mutation-report comparison is non-reproducible
findings.evidence: BUILD_ID=str(uuid.uuid4()) regenerated per run; default mode does a full `json.dumps(sort_keys=True)` byte comparison of candidate vs bundled, INCLUDING volatile build_id + per-mutation duration_seconds + total_duration_seconds. Reproduced: clean replay exits 1 with 'bundled report does not match current run'. Must inject build_id from top-level build and compare only stable fields.

mutation_matrix_semantic_status.note: 13/13 recorded BLOCKED on independent run, but these are the ones whose BLOCKED is genuine vs false-green
